item.title: SoftBank’s bet on OpenAI is starting to weigh
item.description: Japanese group has suffered from recent share falls and a negative outlook from rating agency S&P

item.title: The déjà vu of Europe’s energy shock
item.description: The UK and EU must raise their game on power security

item.title: The new food fad sweeping Korea
item.description: Purchase limits had to be imposed on Dubai chewy cookies

item.title: The UK’s public leadership pipeline has fallen into disrepair
item.description: Institutions are failing to deliver basic commitments because of failures of leadership

item.title: Trump’s Venezuela strategy has failed in Iran
item.description: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei has dashed the US president’s hope of picking Iran’s new leader

item.title: Stagflationary forces are building
item.description: A weakening US labour market and financial strains come as the Iran war and surging oil prices spark concerns about inflation pressures

item.title: A bad jobs report
item.description: Plus, AI and productivity

item.title: Drones: a strategic tool for defence and markets
item.description: Tech groups such as Quantum are winning investor support in Europe, boosting the region’s venture capital industry

item.title: Iran war will leave a complex geoeconomic legacy
item.description: Markets suggest the ramifications are likely to drag on and spread

item.title: You can turn this to your advantage if every news story has ‘tax exile’ in it
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: China’s growth target is a global problem
item.description: Beijing’s export offensive could be more destabilising than Trump’s tariffs

item.title: Latin America’s lithium triangle is now in the hands of the right
item.description: A reversal of political fortune in the region has major implications for critical minerals

item.title: Europe’s impotence extends to energy
item.description: The political momentum behind decarbonising the continent’s energy system has dissipated

item.title: Should we aim for a frictionless world?
item.description: Humanity’s pursuit of speed drives progress, but eliminating delay has risks

item.title: Why California’s plan to tax billionaires is misguided
item.description: In America’s federal system, the rich can easily shift to more competitive states

item.title: Liberals should be less shy about saying what they stand for
item.description: Failure to defend their ideology has created an intellectual vacuum on the American left

item.title: Car insurance bills are going up before they go down
item.description: Costs driven up by combination of skills shortages, supply-chain issues and complicated modern cars

item.title: America chose this war — and must now choose how to end it
item.description: However this conflict concludes, the US and Iran’s new leaders will have to revisit the same issues that sparked hostilities

item.title: Why it’s time to end the grim march of the touchscreen
item.description: Self-service kiosks make us buy more stuff and feel more lonely

item.title: Chart of the Week: Europe’s extra energy tax
item.description: A weaker euro deepens the pain from higher oil prices

item.title: Price is now the main ingredient in the office-lunch slop bowl
item.description: Many chains have raised prices to absorb rising costs — which works as long as customer loyalty, or indifference, endures

item.title: The war of unintended consequences
item.description: Israel and the US have achieved many military aims in Iran, but the unpredictable fallout poses threats to everyone

item.title: We’ll always have Dubai
item.description: Why the newly endangered city will endure

item.title: Allowing 401ks to invest in private markets is a bad move at a bad time
item.description: The move might help asset managers but hurt savers and the economy more broadly

item.title: How mackerel became Schrödinger’s fish
item.description: We sure as hell aren’t buying that much in Waitrose
